papers.title_en: Federated Formal Verification: Cross-Backend Citation, Cross-Axis Convergence, and AI-Orchestrated Proof Dispatch for Production Systems
papers.evidence: 用于联邦形式验证的AI编排证明分发，可应用于硬件验证
papers.tldr: 现有形式化验证将全部义务压入单一证明助手内核，难以适配生产系统的多语言异构需求。本文提出联邦式架构，通过跨后端引用、跨轴收敛与AI编排，将验证活动组织为多语言证明系统。在Mercury高频交易平台的两个生产子系统上验证，将Raft共识的26条公理普查归零，单会话耗时17小时。该架构以轻度DRY原则实现内核间的可论证等价性，在构建系统级抵御漂移。
papers.abstract_en: We propose a federated architecture for production formal verification. Rather than forcing all obligations into a single proof-assistant kernel, the architecture treats a verification campaign as a polyglot proof system composed of three mechanisms: cross-backend citation discharges a TLA+ obligation by citing an equivalent theorem in a structurally distinct kernel, with build- system-level drift-resistance enforced through kernel-level closure-assertion directives; cross-axis convergence composes per-obligation verdicts across independent verifiers into operational kernel-agreement gates; the AI layer is untrusted proof-search labour inside a trusted CI envelope. We validate the architecture on two production subsystems of the Mercury high-frequency-trading platform: a Raft consensus subsystem with full algorithmic scope and a financial-arithmetic invariant layer (balance accounting, automated-market-maker curve invariants, isolated-margin, lock-tracking settlement). The validation campaign reduced a 26-axiom Raft census to zero in 17 active hours of single-session wallclock
